content: After opening a PR, run /greploop until Greptile scores 5/5 with zero unresolved comments, then squash-merge with commit title format '... (#N)'. main is unprotected — verify the build and 'npm test' pass before merging.

All commit messages and PR titles must follow Conventional Commits: type(scope): description, e.g. 'feat(mining): add tool tiers', 'fix: glass pane rendering'. Types: feat, fix, docs, chore, refactor, test, perf, ci, build, style. PR titles use the same format (semantic PRs); since PRs are squash-merged, the PR title becomes the commit title on main, formatted 'type(scope): description (#N)'.

description: Vite dev server served stale modules (old gamemode.ts without new fields) after git branch switches + rebase during a live session, causing phantom E2E failures where new code paths silently no-op'd
resolution: Restart the dev server after heavy git operations (rebase, branch dance); verify served code with: await import('/src/<file>.ts') in the browser console before debugging application logic

description: git rebase origin/main on a branch stacked on a multi-commit feature branch conflicts after squash-merge: patch-ids of the individual commits never match the squash commit (single-commit branches do get skipped)
resolution: Use git rebase --onto origin/main <last-stacked-base-commit> <branch> to replay only the branch's own commits onto the squash-merged main

name: greptile-stale-score-check
description: A Greptile summary comment's confidence score can describe an older commit: it edits one comment per PR and the footer says 'Last reviewed commit: <sha>'. Before acting on a score (especially a drop), compare that footer sha against the PR head — if they differ, the review predates the latest push; check for an in_progress check-run on the head instead of re-triggering
